metadata.title: Why the same API key that shipped your prototype becomes a critical bug in production
metadata.topic: Where a secret is allowed to live
metadata.purpose: The threat model dictates where the secret lives — a bundled key is reverse-engineerable from a shipped app, so production forces it behind a backend boundary, which changes how every request is constructed.
metadata.clock: narration (Kokoro af_kore) — master clock; conform each beat to measured audio
metadata.still_lanes: geo (three credential layers), c2v (dev vs prod request)
metadata.prerequisites: API auth, threat modeling, backend proxy basics
metadata.exclusions: token refresh, OAuth, App Attest attestation mode
metadata.visual_object: Three layers — app binary → backend relay → platform — with the key sliding rightward as the threat surface shrinks
metadata.hook: A bundled key works perfectly in development and is a shipping-app vulnerability the moment you release — the same credential, in the same place, flips from convenient to dangerous.

beats.narration_text: Prototype uses `auth: .apiKey("sk-ant-...")` in source — fine locally. Before App Store release you realize the key is extractable from the binary and must move to `auth: .proxied(headers:[...])`, adding a backend relay that injects the real key server-side.
beats.scaffold_note: from Key case — cold open on the concrete instance

beats.narration_text: Why can't one credential strategy work across both development and production?
A bundled key works perfectly in development and is a shipping-app vulnerability the moment you release — the same credential, in the same place, flips from convenient to dangerous.

The threat model dictates where the secret lives — a bundled key is reverse-engineerable from a shipped app, so production forces it behind a backend boundary, which changes how every request is constructed.
beats.visual: Three layers — app binary → backend relay → platform — with the key sliding rightward as the threat surface shrinks
beats.scaffold_note: the resolving mechanism; orbits the visual object

beats.narration_text: Dev: `.apiKey("sk-ant-...")` in source. Release: `.proxied(headers:["X-App-Token":"..."])` + relay at `yourco.com/claude` that injects the real key.

The threat model dictates where the secret lives — a bundled key is reverse-engineerable from a shipped app, so production forces it behind a backend boundary, which changes how every request is constructed.
